**Sonic Hub World \- Mid April Report**

This document is the mid april report required for the **ICG** class for the ***Sonic Hub World*** Project, made by **Duarte Branco** (nº **119253**).

![][image1]

**repo**: [**https://github.com/duartebranco/sonic-hub-world**](https://github.com/duartebranco/sonic-hub-world)  
**site**: [**https://duartebranco.github.io/sonic-hub-world/**](https://duartebranco.github.io/sonic-hub-world/)

**Objectives**

Recreate the **Sonic Jam’s** (1997, Sega Saturn) **hub world** as a modern, browser-based 3D game built with three.js. Sonic Jam was one of the first Sonic compilations and its standout feature was a free-roaming open world where the player could explore, collect rings, and race around the map. This project reimplements that concept, with some of my own preference design choices.

**What Is Already Done**

**Player and Physics:**  
\- Full momentum physics: tangential-drag turning, slope acceleration, surface-normal jumping, air inertia and variable jump height.  
\- Speed tiers: WALK, RUN and MAX.  
\- Spin-dash: hold Shift/X to charge, release to launch at TOP\_SPEED (bigger than MAX).  
\- Ground dust particles emitted at run speed and above.

**Animation System:**  
\- Three authored animations: idle, walk, and run \- each crafted with the custom \`*animator.html*\` tool.

**World:**  
\- Procedural terrain using layered functions.  
\- Map features: plateaus, ramps, lakes, trenches, a bridge, cliffs, trees, flowers, clouds and a water surface.  
\- Checkerboard path tiles along the intended route.  
\- 22 collectible rings with proximity detection (0.9 units), sparkle particles and HUD counter (ring counter).  
\- Badnik mob meshes placed in the world (patrol AI not yet implemented).

**Camera and HUD (heads up display):**

\- Third-person camera with mouse drag / pointer lock and scroll zoom.  
\- HUD: ring counter, race timer, spin-dash charge bar, speed-lines effect at high speed, Sonic-style font and tittle/loading screen.

**What Still Needs to Be Done**

**Animations:**  
\- Jump animation.  
\- Animation of Sonic being hit by a mob.  
\- Death animation.  
\- Give use of the different facial expressions on those new animations.

**World and Game Logic:**  
\- Deliberate track layout \- the current path is placeholder checkerboard tiles with no intentional design.  
\- Water zone with buoyancy or wading physics; currently the water surface is purely visual.  
\- Enemy interaction: player should take damage on contact with MotoBugs (ring loss, brief invincibility frames) and be able to kill them with a spin attack.  
\- Player hit/death and respawn: hit detection, check if player has rings, and respawn at the last checkpoint.  
\- Checkpoint system to save progress along the route.  
\- Different checkpoints that refer the start and finish line of the track (for the 

**Sound:**  
\- Have a complete soundtrack and use it.  
\- Have sound effects relating to the world and to the actual player (sonic).
